chapter_title.en: Chapter 107 – The merits for the poor Muslims

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/356)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/356)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) الْمَصَائِبُ مِنَحٌ مِنَ اللَّهِ وَ الْفَقْرُ مَخْزُونٌ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Khalid, from his father, from Sa’dan who said,
‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The difficulties are a Conferment from Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, and the poverty is a Hoarded treasure in the Presence of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>’.
en.sarwar: 2. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid from his father from Sa’dan who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) has said, ‘Misfortunes are gifts from Allah and poverty is in His treasure.’”
